name: Linear
description: Productivity reference — dark-first canvas, geometric Inter at every size, indigo-violet signature accent, dense information design. Type is small, leading is tight, hierarchy is encoded in weight and color not size.

--color-midnight-void.role: Primary surface — page background, the canvas everything sits on
--color-midnight-void.context: near-black with a microscopic cool tint

--color-shadow-surface.role: Elevated dark surface — card fills, modal backgrounds, sidebars
--color-shadow-surface.context: lifted dark grey, the second-elevation step

--color-cloud-whisper.role: Primary text on midnight-void — headlines, key UI labels
--color-cloud-whisper.context: off-white, calibrated to not glare on near-black

--color-violet-spark.role: Signature accent — CTAs, active states, focus rings, brand-defining hue
--color-violet-spark.context: Linear indigo-violet, the brand's identity color

--color-ash-divider.role: Hairline dividers, list separators, low-emphasis borders

--font-display-inter.role: Display headlines — hero, section titles, marketing-page H1

--font-body-inter.role: Body copy, UI chrome, list items, navigation

antiPatterns: Body text must never exceed 16px — Linear hierarchy is encoded in weight (500 vs 400) and color (cloud-whisper vs graphite-body), not in size. Large body type breaks the productivity character.
Never apply --color-violet-spark to large surface fills — Linear uses it as a 1–3% accent (button bg, active state, focus ring). As a section background it looks like a generic gradient SaaS.
Never use serif typography anywhere — the Linear voice is monoculturally Inter. A serif headline reads as a different brand entirely.
Avoid drop-shadows on dark surfaces — Linear separates layers via color-step (midnight-void → shadow-surface → ash-divider), never shadow blur. Soft shadows on dark mode look like Notion, not Linear.
